text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. Readable code is like a well-written story that anyone can understand. The first step is following naming conventions; use clear and meaningful names for variables, functions, and classes. For example, instead of `a` or `temp`, use `userAge` or `totalPrice`. Break your code into small functions that each do one specific task. This makes your code easier to test and reuse. Finally, always think about maintainability. Future developers should be able to read your code and make changes without confusion. By writing clean code, you save time and avoid headaches later.
